subject: Chemistry
title: Marie Curie Discovers Radium
description: A short lesson to help you apply Marie Curie Discovers Radium.

hook.text: Marie Curie processed eight tons of pitchblende ore by hand to isolate one-tenth of a gram of radium.

buildup.text: She noticed that uranium ore was more radioactive than pure uranium. Something else must be hiding inside.

discovery.text: In 1898, she and Pierre isolated two new elements: polonium (named for Poland) and radium ('ray').

twist.text: Radium glowed blue in the dark. People put it in paint, water, and even toothpaste before knowing the danger.

climax.text: Curie won two Nobel Prizes—the only person to win in two different sciences. She died of radiation exposure.

punchline.text: Eight tons of ore, one-tenth of a gram. Two Nobel Prizes.

quiz.question: How did Curie realize there were unknown elements in uranium ore?
options: The ore was more radioactive than pure uranium alone could explain
The ore glowed in the dark
The ore was heavier than expected
